title: Discard mechanism for tile-based rendering
abstract: A tile identifier may be assigned to tiles processed in order in a pixel shader. When the tiles are processed out of order in the pixel shader, the tile identifier may be used to determine when rendering is complete and a tile may be discarded.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1 . A method comprising: assigning a tile identifier to tiles processed in order in a pixel shader; processing the tiles out of order in the pixel shader; and using the tile identifier in the pixel shader to determine when rendering is complete for a tile and a tile may be discarded. 2. The method of claim 1 further including receiving an access request in a first level cache with a marker that indicates that all pixels in a tile were rasterized. 3. The method of claim 2 including sending a discard indicator to indicate whether a surface needs to be written out after a rendering pass completes. 4. The method of claim 3 including sending a completion indicator to indicate whether writes for the tile to a next level cache have completed. 5. The method of claim 4 including sending a set or reset signal for the completion indicator to the next level cache. 6. The method of claim 5 including upon an eviction of a cacheline in the next level cache, determining whether to write back to memory based on whether both the discard and completion indicators. 7. The method of claim 4 including tracking per stream tile identifiers in said caches. 8. The method of claim 7 including tracking whether a stream is discardable on a per cacheline basis. 9. One or more non-transitory computer readable media storing instructions to perform a sequence comprising: 

 assigning a tile identifier to tiles processed in order in a pixel shader; processing the tiles out of order in the pixel shader; and using the tile identifier in the pixel shader to determine when rendering is complete for a tile and a tile may be discarded. 10. The media of claim 9 further storing instructions to perform a sequence including receiving an access request in a first level cache with a marker that indicates that all pixels in a tile were rasterized. 1 1 .
